qa.answer: Set a single primary objective per campaign (awareness, traffic, leads, sales) and align the platform objective accordingly so algorithms optimize to the true goal. Define SMART targets and the KPIs that prove success by funnel stage (impressions/reach/video views for awareness; CTR/CPC for engagement; CVR/CPA/ROAS for conversion).
implication: Map each campaign to one objective and KPI set before launch.
Document SMART goals and acceptable KPI ranges per funnel stage.
Avoid mixed goals in one campaign to prevent optimization drift.

questions: Topic: Platform selection – demand capture before demand gen
qa.answer: Prioritize bottom‑funnel demand capture (Google Search; optionally Microsoft Ads) before scaling top‑funnel social/video. Search captures high intent (Google ~93% share vs Bing ~3%) while social generates demand and reach (Meta broad B2C; LinkedIn precise B2B but higher CPC; TikTok/Snap for Gen Z; Pinterest discovery; Reddit niche communities; Amazon essential for marketplace sales).
implication: Ensure brand and core generic search coverage before heavy prospecting.
Choose channels by audience‑objective fit, not habit.
Layer retail media if you sell on marketplaces.

qa.answer: Allocate enough budget per ad set/campaign to exit learning (~50 conversion events/week on Meta). SMB starting ranges: Google $1k–$10k/mo, Meta $1k–$5k/mo, then reallocate to the best cost‑per‑result. Underfunding causes unstable delivery and noisy signals.
implication: Back‑solve budgets from CPA targets and needed weekly conversions.
Consolidate small ad sets to concentrate learning.
Scale budgets in 15–30% steps to avoid resets.

qa.answer: Mirror funnel stages: separate Prospecting vs Retargeting. On Meta, organize by audience type with multiple creatives; on Search, split brand vs generic and group by keyword themes. Clear separation avoids internal competition and clarifies optimization levers.
implication: Use distinct campaigns for prospecting and retargeting.
Split brand and generic search for bid/ROAS control.
Align ad groups/ad sets to logical product, geo, or segment themes.

qa.answer: Let campaigns run long enough for data (≥2 weeks learning; 4–8 weeks to optimize). For evergreen demand, run always‑on (esp. search). Refresh creatives roughly monthly or when frequency >5–6 and performance dips. Batch substantive changes to avoid learning resets.
implication: Schedule refreshes and batch edits to protect learning.
Pre‑build replacement creatives to avoid fatigue gaps.
Flight for fixed‑date pushes; keep search always‑on.

qa.answer: Define complementary roles across platforms: social/video to generate interest, search/Pinterest to capture intent, retargeting to convert. Reuse audience signals cross‑channel (e.g., LinkedIn leads → Meta retargeting; Meta engagers → Google Display similar). Sequence creatives (awareness video → product carousel to viewers) to move users down the funnel.
implication: Share audience lists across platforms where policy allows.
Build sequenced journeys tied to engagement depth.
Maintain message consistency across channels and stages.

qa.answer: Use a 70‑20‑10 portfolio: ~70% proven 'bread‑and‑butter', 20% scaling initiatives (new audiences/formats that already show promise), 10% experimental (emerging platforms/ideas). This protects core performance while institutionalizing learning.
implication: Ring‑fence 10% for experimentation; don’t raid it.
Define promotion criteria to graduate 10% → 20% → 70%.
Publish monthly migration decisions and learnings.

qa.answer: Define personas and intent tiers, but avoid over‑layering filters that shrink delivery. Meta often prefers broader audiences with optimized delivery. Start broad, then refine based on conversion data and breakdowns (age, geo, placement).
implication: Monitor audience size indicators; avoid 'too specific' warnings.
Use broad + conversion optimization as a default starting point.
Refine with exclusions and creative match, not excessive filters.

qa.answer: Use high‑quality seed data (top customers/SQOs/purchasers) for lookalikes. Start with 1–2% for efficiency; expand to 3–10% to scale. Platform AI expansion can further broaden while maintaining performance.
implication: Continuously refresh seeds with recent high‑value cohorts.
Test multiple lookalike sizes and sources.
Exclude source audiences to prevent overlap.

qa.answer: Retargeting drives outsized ROI (10× CTR vs display; +70% conversion likelihood). Build behavioral segments (viewed product, ATC‑no purchase, high‑intent browsers) and use tailored ads (dynamic product ads). Cap frequency (3–5/week) to avoid overexposure and exclude recent converters.
implication: Implement complete pixel/tag coverage and event taxonomy.
Segment by intent and time‑window; tailor creatives/offers.
Apply frequency caps and converter exclusions.

qa.answer: Use exclusions to preserve purpose per campaign: exclude customers from acquisition; exclude site visitors from lookalike seeds; exclude employees/irrelevant groups. This reduces cannibalization and focuses budget on incremental reach.
implication: Audit audience overlaps regularly.
Maintain a global exclusion list (customers, staff, partners).
Exclude converters from active lower‑funnel campaigns.

qa.answer: Cover high‑ and mid‑intent keywords on search (use match types and negatives). On Display/YouTube, target topics/placements. Reddit is primarily contextual (subreddits/interests) and requires native‑feeling creative aligned to community norms.
implication: Maintain a living negative keyword list.
Curate placement/topic allow‑lists for Display/YouTube.
Adapt tone/format to subreddit culture when advertising on Reddit.

qa.answer: Use demographic filters only when strongly indicated; otherwise learn from data then constrain. Geo target priority regions and use bid adjustments or regional splits when performance diverges. Daypart based on conversion by hour/day and lead handling SLAs.
implication: Run breakdowns to find high‑value demos/regions before narrowing.
Apply ad schedule bid modifiers where supported.
Align business hours/sales response with ad scheduling.

qa.answer: Leverage life‑event and in‑market signals where relevant (recently moved, new parents, in‑market shopping). Use judiciously for offers that naturally fit the life moment to lift relevance and CVR.
implication: Test event‑aligned creatives and offers (e.g., 'new home' bundles).
Combine with geo/time cues (seasonal spikes, local context).
Exclude irrelevant life‑events to avoid waste.

qa.answer: Enable all relevant placements/devices to start; let algorithms optimize, then trim clear underperformers (e.g., some Audience Network apps). On Microsoft Ads, optional LinkedIn profile targeting can layer B2B dimensions onto search.
implication: Monitor placement reports; exclude persistently poor placements.
Use Advantage+ placements unless data compels constraints.
Segment by device only when performance diverges meaningfully.

questions: Topic: Privacy, first‑party data & server‑side signals
qa.answer: Post‑iOS14 and cookie deprecation shift value to first‑party data and server‑side conversions (Meta CAPI, Google Enhanced Conversions). Expect smaller remarketing pools; use broader signals and modeled conversions. Topics API and aggregated measurement help recover signal.
implication: Build consented lists; encourage logins/newsletter opt‑ins.
Implement CAPI/Enhanced Conversions and GA4 properly.
Plan for modeled attribution and validate with lift/geo tests.

qa.answer: Thumb‑stopping visuals with clear focal points perform. Human faces often lift recall/CTR. Use motion (short videos, subtle animations) to catch attention in feeds. Keep assets native‑looking to platform culture for authenticity.
implication: Design with a strong focal point and high contrast.
Prioritize people‑centric creative for social where relevant.
Add lightweight motion variants to static concepts.

qa.answer: Lead with value proposition and CTA. On search, include the keyword in headlines and use extensions. Match tone to audience and platform; keep text concise and scannable. Always clarify next action (Shop, Learn, Sign Up).
implication: Template headline/value/CTA variants for rapid testing.
Enable all relevant extensions (sitelinks, callouts, snippets).
Mirror ad promises explicitly on the landing page.

questions: Topic: Video ads – length, hook & captions
qa.answer: Favor 5–15s videos for most social placements; hook in first 3 seconds. Add captions/text overlays for mute viewers yet design sound‑on for enhanced engagement. Use vertical 9:16 for Stories/Reels/TikTok; auto‑resize tools can assist.
implication: Front‑load the value proposition and visual hook.
Produce vertical and square cuts alongside landscape.
Caption all videos; test music/sfx variants.

questions: Topic: Formats – carousel, collection, lead forms, discovery
qa.answer: Use carousels to showcase ranges or stepwise stories; collections/Instant Experiences for mobile storefronts; native lead forms for frictionless lead gen; Discovery/Responsive Display with rich assets for broad reach. Interactive formats (quizzes, AR) can materially lift engagement.
implication: Map format to objective and decision stage.
Keep lead forms short; set fast follow‑up SLAs.
Test interactive units where fit (e.g., Pinterest Quiz, AR lenses).

qa.answer: Design for mobile first (most social ad exposure), using large type, vertical aspect where appropriate, and fast loads. Respect platform policies, especially around personal attributes and sensitive claims, to avoid disapprovals and delivery loss.
implication: Preview all assets on device; validate legibility on small screens.
Implement lightweight, fast landing pages (Core Web Vitals).
Pre‑review copy against platform policy checklists.

qa.answer: Always test multiple creative variants. Use Dynamic Creative/Responsive formats to mix and match components. Review asset‑level reports to scale winners. Leverage platform creative automation (e.g., Meta Advantage+ Creative) for resizing/templating gains.
implication: Set a weekly cadence to launch/retire creative variants.
Centralize learnings: concepts, angles, hooks that lowered CPA.
Adopt automation for speed while keeping brand QA gates.

qa.answer: Budget from goals (e.g., target conversions × CPA). Prefer concentration over fragmentation to reach statistical power. Use lifetime budgets for flexible pacing; daily for control. Increase budgets gradually (15–30%) to avoid volatility.
implication: Define minimum spend floors to reach MDE per test.
Use rules to throttle/boost based on KPI thresholds.
Watch spend pacing mid‑month to avoid end‑period shortfalls.

qa.answer: Default to automated bidding (Target CPA/ROAS, Max Conversions/Value; Meta Lowest Cost) once sufficient data exists (>15–20 conversions/month; ideally >50/week). Use manual/caps only for constraints or known edge cases. Value‑based bidding prioritizes revenue over volume when fed accurate values.
implication: Feed clean conversion events and values; audit regularly.
Avoid overly tight caps that throttle delivery.
Raise/lower targets based on sustained actual performance.

qa.answer: Track CPC, CPM, CPA, ROAS/ACoS and compare to reasonable ranges (e.g., FB avg CPC ~$.50; CPM ~$8–12; Google Search CPC industry‑dependent; some keywords $100+). Improve Quality Score/relevance to lower CPC and raise rank without brute‑force bids.
implication: Benchmark by country/industry; monitor deltas not absolutes.
Improve ad/keyword/landing alignment to lift QS.
Investigate sudden CPM spikes for relevance or seasonality.

qa.answer: Use data‑driven attribution where available; understand platform modeling. Complement with lift tests (geo/holdout) and path analysis to value assist roles. Reallocate budget on marginal incremental ROAS, considering diminishing returns and cross‑channel synergies.
implication: Standardize attribution windows and compare apples‑to‑apples.
Run periodic lift tests to validate modeled performance.
Shift dollars toward highest marginal iROAS, not just lowest CPC.

qa.answer: Protect brand search; sustain remarketing; focus on high‑intent queries and 1–2% lookalikes when budget‑constrained. Exclude money‑wasting placements (e.g., specific mobile app categories) and maintain negative keyword hygiene.
implication: Always‑on brand search and remarketing as baseline.
Aggressively prune poor placements and irrelevant queries.
Prioritize segments with proven high CVR/AOV when funds are tight.

qa.answer: Define and track stage‑appropriate metrics: awareness (impressions, reach, frequency, viewable impressions, video view rates); engagement/traffic (CTR, CPC, session quality); conversion (conversions, CPA, ROAS/ROI). Monitor video metrics (ThruPlay, CPV) and on‑site behavior (bounce/time).
implication: Use UTMs consistently to analyze by campaign/ad in GA4.
Add stage‑specific annotations to dashboards for context.
Tie media KPIs to business outcomes (orders, pipeline, CLV).

questions: Topic: Tracking setup – pixels, GA4, GTM
qa.answer: Install and validate all platform pixels (Meta, Google, TikTok, LinkedIn, Pinterest, Snapchat). Use GA4 for event‑based analytics and attribution, managed via GTM for governance. Avoid duplicate fires (esp. purchase). Import offline conversions for lead quality optimization.
implication: Maintain a tag inventory and QA checklist; test end‑to‑end.
Configure GA4 conversions and link to Google Ads.
Pass enhanced match parameters (email/phone hashed) where allowed.

qa.answer: Expect discrepancies across platforms vs analytics due to attribution, privacy, and modeling. Implement server‑side tracking (CAPI/Enhanced Conversions) to recover 5–10%+ attribution on privacy‑impacted traffic and improve optimization signals.
implication: Compare 'observed' vs 'modeled' conversions in reports.
Use consent mode where required to model gaps.
Rely on triangulation: platform, analytics, and lift tests.

qa.answer: Work a continuous loop: Measure → Analyze → Adjust → Test. Change one major lever at a time to isolate impact. Use formal experiments (Google Experiments, Meta Experiments) to validate big changes and wait for statistical power (~50 conversions or ≥1 week).
implication: Log all changes with timestamps and hypotheses.
Define MDE/power per test to prevent premature calls.
Use platform experiments for controlled splits when feasible.

qa.answer: Refresh creatives every 2–4 weeks depending on spend/audience size; TikTok/Snap fatigue faster than Meta. Run overlap to A/B rather than hard swaps. Scale creative angles that reduce CPA and archive learnings usable cross‑platform.
implication: Track frequency and performance decay to trigger refreshes.
Maintain a rolling pipeline of assets by platform/aspect ratio.
Repurpose winners to new formats (shorts/reels/carousels).

qa.answer: Use breakdowns and reports to trim poor segments: Search Terms (negatives), Placement reports (exclusions), Audience breakdowns (age/geo/placement). Focus budgets on segments with superior CVR/CPA/ROAS and adequate scale.
implication: Review reports weekly; act on repeat underperformers.
Exclude accidental‑click app categories where needed.
Duplicate winning segments into new geos/devices to scale.

qa.answer: Align landing content tightly with ad promise; test headlines, layout, social proof, and CTAs. Mobile speed is critical (aim <3s; <2s ideal). LP wins reduce CPA more reliably than bid tweaks alone.
implication: Run LP A/B tests alongside ad tests; share learnings.
Audit Core Web Vitals; compress assets and optimize images.
Match keyword and creative language on the LP.

qa.answer: Meta: fix disapprovals, enlarge too‑small audiences, loosen caps, increase budget to exit learning, check account/campaign spend limits. Google: inspect Impression Share Lost (Budget/Rank), raise budget/bids or improve Quality Score; resolve negative/keyword conflicts.
implication: Use platform diagnostics and change history to pinpoint causes.
Temporarily switch to lowest‑cost to validate delivery headroom.
Duplicate ad sets/campaigns when resets are needed.

qa.answer: Increase budgets gradually; expand lookalike sizes (1% → 3–5%), enable audience expansion, add related keywords, and raise CPA/ROAS targets slightly to unlock volume. Scale to new geos; port winning concepts across platforms (e.g., Spark/UGC on TikTok/Meta).
implication: Track marginal CPA/ROAS; stop when diminishing returns bite.
Ensure ops (inventory, sales) can handle scaled demand.
Localize/translate when expanding internationally.

questions: Topic: Automation & AI – PMax, Advantage+, TikTok ACO
qa.answer: Adopt Google Performance Max (with negatives and search themes), Meta Advantage+ (Shopping, Creative) and TikTok Smart Performance/ACO to simplify setup and access AI optimizations. Use insights to steer creative strategy while feeding accurate goals and audience signals.
implication: Provide high‑quality assets and audience signals to AI.
Add campaign‑level negatives where supported to prevent waste.
Monitor asset group/ad set insights; iterate creative accordingly.

questions: Topic: New surfaces – retail media, TikTok search, CTV
qa.answer: Retail media (Amazon, Walmart, Target) grows with high purchase intent; optimize feeds and consider video in search results. TikTok Search Ads open keyword intent within TikTok. CTV (YouTube on TV, Hulu, Roku) offers biddable large‑screen reach with digital targeting.
implication: Expand measurement to include retail media and CTV outcomes.
Produce vertical/short‑form assets adaptable to TV and social.
Test TikTok search keywords aligned to organic behavior.

questions: Topic: Creative trends – UGC, interactive & AR
qa.answer: UGC/testimonial styles improve authenticity and engagement. Interactive units (polls, quizzes) and AR try‑ons can materially lift interaction and conversion for applicable categories (beauty, apparel, furniture).
implication: Source UGC via creators/customers with proper disclosures.
Pilot AR where product fit is strong; measure lift vs static.
Use Spark/whitelisted creator posts to boost native performance.

questions: Topic: Analytics innovations – GA4, MMM, consent mode
qa.answer: GA4 enables cross‑platform event analytics and predictive metrics; link to Google Ads. Lightweight MMM (e.g., Robyn) complements attribution by estimating incremental channel contributions. Consent Mode and enhanced conversions model performance under consent constraints.
implication: Link GA4↔Google Ads; import conversions and audiences.
Use MMM alongside attribution for budget planning.
Implement consent mode where applicable to recover modeled conversions.

qa.answer: Expect ad costs to vary with macro conditions and seasonal competition. Adjust budgets, offers, and channel mix dynamically. Track policy changes (e.g., under‑18 restrictions, sensitive targeting removal) to avoid delivery issues and maintain compliance.
implication: Pre‑plan seasonal budget ramps and protective guardrails.
Maintain a policy watchlist and remediation playbook.
Diversify channels to hedge auction competition spikes.

qa.answer: Performance often hinges on pricing, offers, sales follow‑up, and on‑site UX. Coordinate with product/sales/web to improve conversion and LTV (e.g., live chat, email/SMS nurturing, bundles). Media optimization and post‑click optimization must move in lockstep.
implication: Share media insights with sales/product weekly.
Stand up nurture flows for leads from native lead forms.
Track LTV/CAC by source to inform true scaling decisions.
